intro: Gymnanthes multiramea, also known as the "Wild Jasmine" or "Palo de Vaca", is a shrub native to the tropical forests of Central and South America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of wounds, fever, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Gymnanthes multiramea include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Gymnanthes multiramea is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture to harness its health benefits.
